title: var Hoisting vs let TDZ
explanation: var name is hoisted with undefined. let age is in the TDZ — accessing before declaration throws ReferenceError.
mentalModel: var hoists with undefined. let/const hoist but stay in the TDZ until the declaration line

title: Function Declaration vs Expression Hoisting
output:
declaration
TypeError: bar is not a function
explanation: Function declarations hoist fully (name + body). Function expressions with var hoist only the variable as undefined — calling undefined() is a TypeError.
mentalModel: Declarations hoist fully. Expressions hoist only the variable (as undefined)

title: Block Scoping — let Inside if
explanation: The let inside if is block-scoped to those { } braces. Since the condition is false, it never executes. console.log sees the outer greeting = 'Hey!'.
mentalModel: let/const are trapped inside the nearest { } braces. They can't leak out like var

title: var Redeclaration — Last Value Wins
explanation: var allows redeclaration — the latest value wins. let/const throw SyntaxError on redeclaration.
mentalModel: var allows redeclaration (last value wins). let/const throw on redeclaration

title: IIFE and Accidental Globals
explanation: y = 10 has no let/var/const → accidental global. let x is block-scoped to the IIFE. Outside, x doesn't exist (typeof = 'undefined'), but y is on global (typeof = 'number').
mentalModel: Assignment without declaration = accidental global. let stays block-scoped

title: catch Block Scope Shadowing
explanation: catch(x) creates a block-scoped x that shadows the outer x. Inside catch, x=1 modifies the local x. But y is not redeclared → y=3 modifies outer y.
mentalModel: catch parameters are block-scoped and shadow outer variables of the same name

title: TDZ — let Shadows Outer Even Before Declaration
explanation: let name inside getName shadows the outer name. But accessing it before declaration = TDZ ReferenceError. JS sees the inner let and won't look at outer scope.
mentalModel: If a let/const exists anywhere in the block, it shadows outer variables from the start

title: const TDZ in Function Scope
explanation: Same TDZ issue as let — const randomValue inside getInfo shadows the outer one. Accessing typeof before the declaration line still throws ReferenceError because the variable is in the TDZ.
mentalModel: typeof does NOT save you from the TDZ. const/let in the same block = TDZ from block start

title: 'use strict' Prevents Implicit Globals
explanation: In strict mode, you MUST declare variables before assigning to them. age = 21 without var/let/const throws ReferenceError. In sloppy mode, it would create an accidental global.
mentalModel: 'use strict' prevents accidental globals — assignment without declaration = ReferenceError

title: delete on var vs Global Property
explanation: Variables declared with var/const/let cannot be deleted — delete returns false. But 'age' is an undeclared assignment (accidental global property), which CAN be deleted — returns true.
mentalModel: delete works on object properties, not on declared variables. Accidental globals are deletable

title: Return on New Line — ASI Trap
output:
a is bigger
undefined
b is bigger
undefined
explanation: Automatic Semicolon Insertion (ASI) adds a semicolon after 'return' because a+b is on a new line. The function always returns undefined. The if/else still runs, but the return value is lost.
mentalModel: Never put a newline after 'return'. ASI will insert a semicolon and your return value is lost
